rag_rules: Place AGREE contracts in component type annexes for port-level assumptions and guarantees, and in implementation annexes for subcomponent bindings.
Ensure that AGREE annex content respects AADL scope rules: use only visible identifiers and avoid package-qualified names not in the model.
generic_rules: Use component type annexes for assumptions and guarantees that reference component ports.
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation annexes after relevant subcomponents and connections.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or data types not present in the analyzed AADL model.
Only include AGREE clauses that reference visible features or constants from the AADL model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assumptions and guarantees should reference only visible identifiers from the AADL model.
Constants and equations in AGREE should match the data types of the corresponding AADL features.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-level bindings in component type annexes.
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or AADL properties as AGREE variables.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE syntax and annex placement rules
